Vulnerabilities.Title: AutoMapper Vulnerable to Denial of Service (DoS) via Uncontrolled Recursion
Vulnerabilities.Description: AutoMapper is a convention-based object-object mapper in .NET. Versions prior to 15.1.1 and 16.1.1 are vulnerable to a Denial of Service (DoS) attack. When mapping deeply nested object graphs, the library uses recursive method calls without enforcing a default maximum depth limit. This allows an attacker to provide a specially crafted object graph that exhausts the thread's stack memory, triggering a `StackOverflowException` and causing the entire application process to terminate. Versions 15.1.1 and 16.1.1 fix the issue.

Vulnerabilities.Title: dotnet: Information Disclosure: MD.SqlClient(MDS) & System.data.SQLClient (SDS)
Vulnerabilities.Description: Microsoft.Data.SqlClient and System.Data.SqlClient SQL Data Provider Security Feature Bypass Vulnerability

Vulnerabilities.Title: Improper Handling of Exceptional Conditions in Newtonsoft.Json
Vulnerabilities.Description: Newtonsoft.Json before version 13.0.1 is affected by a mishandling of exceptional conditions vulnerability. Crafted data that is passed to the JsonConvert.DeserializeObject method may trigger a StackOverflow exception resulting in denial of service. Depending on the usage of the library, an unauthenticated and remote attacker may be able to cause the denial of service condition.

Vulnerabilities.Title: Apache log4net versions before 2.0.10 do not disable XML external enti ...
Vulnerabilities.Description: Apache log4net versions before 2.0.10 do not disable XML external entities when parsing log4net configuration files. This allows for XXE-based attacks in applications that accept attacker-controlled log4net configuration files.
